name: Interp the Aiming Rotation value to achieve smooth aiming rotation changes. Interpolating the rotation before calculating the angle ensures the value is not affected by changes in actor rotation, allowing slow aiming rotation changes with fast actor rotation changes.
title: Interp the Aiming Rotation value to achieve smooth aiming rotation changes. Interpolating the rotation before calculating the angle ensures the value is not affected by changes in actor rotation, allowing slow aiming rotation changes with fast actor rotation changes.

name: Calculate the Aiming angle and Smoothed Aiming Angle by getting the delta between the aiming rotation and the actor rotation.
title: Calculate the Aiming angle and Smoothed Aiming Angle by getting the delta between the aiming rotation and the actor rotation.
terms: Calculate the Aiming angle and Smoothed Aiming Angle by getting the delta between the aiming rotation and the actor rotation.

name: Use the Aiming Yaw Angle divided by the number of spine+pelvis bones to get the amount of spine rotation needed to remain facing the camera direction.
title: Use the Aiming Yaw Angle divided by the number of spine+pelvis bones to get the amount of spine rotation needed to remain facing the camera direction.
terms: Use the Aiming Yaw Angle divided by the number of spine+pelvis bones to get the amount of spine rotation needed to remain facing the camera direction.

name: Get the delta between the Movement Input rotation and Actor rotation and map it to a range of 0-1. This value is used in the aim offset behavior to make the character look toward the Movement Input.
title: Get the delta between the Movement Input rotation and Actor rotation and map it to a range of 0-1. This value is used in the aim offset behavior to make the character look toward the Movement Input.

name: Separate the Aiming Yaw Angle into 3 separate Yaw Times. These 3 values are used in the Aim Offset behavior to improve the blending of the aim offset when rotating completely around the character. This allows you to keep the aiming responsive but still smoothly blend from left to right or right to left.
title: Separate the Aiming Yaw Angle into 3 separate Yaw Times. These 3 values are used in the Aim Offset behavior to improve the blending of the aim offset when rotating completely around the character. This allows you to keep the aiming responsive but still smoothly blend from left to right or right to left.

name: Clamp the Aiming Pitch Angle to a range of 1 to 0 for use in the vertical aim sweeps.
title: Clamp the Aiming Pitch Angle to a range of 1 to 0 for use in the vertical aim sweeps.
terms: Clamp the Aiming Pitch Angle to a range of 1 to 0 for use in the vertical aim sweeps.

name: Update the fall speed. Setting this value only while in the air allows you to use it within the AnimGraph for the landing strength. If not, the Z velocity would return to 0 on landing.

title: Interp and set the In Air Lean Amount

name: Get the Aim Offset weight by getting the opposite of the Aim Offset Mask.
title: Get the Aim Offset weight by getting the opposite of the Aim Offset Mask.

name: Blend and set the Hand IK weights to ensure they only are weighted if allowed by the Arm layers.
title: Blend and set the Hand IK weights to ensure they only are weighted if allowed by the Arm layers.
terms: Blend and set the Hand IK weights to ensure they only are weighted if allowed by the Arm layers.

name: Set whether the arms should blend in mesh space or local space. The Mesh space weight will always be 1 unless the Local Space (LS) curve is fully weighted.
title: Set whether the arms should blend in mesh space or local space. The Mesh space weight will always be 1 unless the Local Space (LS) curve is fully weighted.
terms: Set whether the arms should blend in mesh space or local space. The Mesh space weight will always be 1 unless the Local Space (LS) curve is fully weighted.
